etymology.story: 1650s, "occurring without external stimulus," from Late Latin spontaneus "willing, of one's free will," from Latin (sua) sponte "of one's own accord, willingly;" of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 遥远的， 偏僻的； 关系疏远的； 脱离的； 绝少的； 微乎其微的； 孤高的， 冷淡的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French remot or directly from Latin remotus "afar off, remote, distant in place," past participle of removere "move back or away" (see remove (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 破裂 + 行为/过程/结果

meanings.definitionCn: 失事，残骸；精神或身体已垮的人
未经开发的， 未经使用的， 未经触动的； 处女的

etymology.story: late 14c., "to cast down," from Old French aflicter, from Latin afflictare "to damage, harass, torment," frequentative of affligere (past participle afflictus) "to dash down, overthrow," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + fligere (past participle flictus) "to strike," from PIE root *bhlig- "to strike" (source also of Greek phlibein "to press, crush," Czech blizna "scar," Welsh blif "catapult").
mid-14c., reprochen "to rebuke, reproach," from  Anglo-French repruchier, Old French reprochier "upbraid, blame, accuse, speak ill of," from reproche (see reproach (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 忧郁的， 沮丧的； 令人沮丧的； 昏暗的， 阴沉的

etymology.story: 1580s, probably from gloom (n.) even though that word is not attested as early as this one.
late 13c., "to keep safe from danger," from Old North French warantir "safeguard, protect; guarantee, pledge" (Old French garantir), from warant (see warrant (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 计算， 推算； 计划， 打算

etymology.story: 1560s, "to compute, to estimate by mathematical means," from Latin calculatus, past participle of calculare "to reckon, compute," from calculus (see calculus).
"give formal approval to," late 14c., autorisen, from Old French autoriser "authorize, give authority to" (12c.), from Medieval Latin auctorizare, from auctor (see author (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 听众， 观众； 读者

etymology.story: late 14c., "the action of hearing," from Old French audience, from Latin audentia "a hearing, listening," from audientum (nominative audiens), present participle of audire "to hear," from PIE compound *au-dh- "to perceive physically, grasp," from root *au- "to perceive" (source also of Greek aisthanesthai "to feel;" Sanskrit avih, Avestan avish "openly, evidently;" Old Church Slavonic javiti "to reveal").

root.meaning: to say over again, repeat what has already been said or written

morphology.mnemonic: to say over again, repeat what has already been said or written + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., "restatement, repetition of the words of another," from rehearse + -al (2), or from Old French rehearsal "a repeating." Sense in theater and music, "act of rehearsing," is from 1570s.

meanings.definitionCn: 以鼻吸气，用力吸入；嗅，闻
叙述文， 故事； 叙述， 讲述

etymology.story: "a tale, story," 1560s, from Middle French narrative and from narrative (adj.)..

late 14c., "to hold, occupy, reside in" (without regard to ownership), a back formation from possession and in part from Old French possesser "to have and hold, take, be in possession of" (mid-13c.), from Latin possess-, past participle stem of possidere "to have and hold, possess, be master of, own," from posse "to be able," from potis "able, powerful" (see potent) + esse "to be" (see be).

meanings.definitionCn: 同时发生； 相符， 相一致； 位置重合， 重叠

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 落入/巧合

etymology.story: 1705, "be identical in substance or nature," but from 1640s as a verb in English in Latin form, "occupy the same space, agree in position," from Medieval Latin coincidere (used in astrology), literally "to fall upon together," from Latin com- "together" (see co-) + incidere "to fall upon" (in- "upon" + cadere "to fall;" see case (n.1)).
mid-15c., "one who remains neutral," from Latin neutralis "of neuter gender," (see neutral (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 抓痕， 划痕； 抓， 搔， 刮
分析， 分析报告； 分解， 解析

etymology.story: 1580s, "resolution of anything complex into simple elements" (opposite of synthesis), from Medieval Latin analysis (15c.), from Greek analysis "a breaking up, a loosening, releasing," noun of action from analyein "unloose, release, set free; to loose a ship from its moorings," in Aristotle, "to analyze," from ana "up, throughout" (see ana-) + lysis "a loosening," from lyein "to unfasten" (see lose).
early 13c., "to form, fashion;" c.

meanings.definitionCn: 有合作意向的，乐意合作的；合作的，协作的

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 工作 + 使…/具有…的 + 有…倾向的

meanings.definitionCn: 三部分的，三方的；三倍的，三重的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French triple or directly from Latin triplus (see triple (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 离合器； 掌握， 控制； 把握， 抓紧

etymology.story: Old English clyccan "bring together, bend (the fingers), clench," from PIE *klukja- (source also of Swedish klyka "clamp, fork;" related to cling).

meanings.definitionCn: 调和， 使缓和； 回火
暗中摸索，搜索；探索，搜寻
感到高兴， 充满喜悦
实物， 物体； 目的， 目标； 对象， 客体； 宾语
谦逊的；地位低下的；简陋的

etymology.story: late 14c., "render oneself humble" (intrans.), also "to bend, kneel or bow;" late 15c.

Old English clif "rock, promontory, steep slope," from Proto-Germanic *kliban (source also of Old Saxon clif, Old Norse klif, Middle Dutch klippe, Dutch klip, Old High German klep, German Klippe "cliff, promontory, steep rock").

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 结束/限制 + ite

etymology.story: late 14c., "eternal, limitless," also "extremely great in number," from Old French infinit "endless, boundless" and directly from Latin infinitus "unbounded, unlimited, countless, numberless," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + finitus "defining, definite," from finis "end" (see finish (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 痛苦， 苦恼， 苦难； 悲惨的境遇， 贫苦

morphology.mnemonic: a cumin seed splitter. + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: late 14c., "condition of external unhappiness," from Old French misere "miserable situation, misfortune, distress" (12c.), from Latin miseria "wretchedness," from miser (see miser).

meanings.definitionCn: 承认，承认…为真；承认失败；允许，让予

etymology.story: 1630s, from Middle French concéder or directly from Latin concedere "give way, yield, go away, depart, retire," figuratively "agree, consent, give precedence," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + cedere "to go, grant, give way" (see cede).

meanings.definitionCn: 值得注意的，显著的；著名的

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French notable "well-known, notable, remarkable" (13c.) and directly from Latin notabilis "noteworthy, extraordinary," from notare "to note" (see note (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 似有道理的， 似乎正确的， 貌似可信的

etymology.story: 1540s, "acceptable, agreeable," from Latin plausibilis "deserving applause, acceptable," from plaus-, past participle stem of plaudere "to applaud" (see plaudit).
late 14c., from Medieval Latin aequator (diei et noctis) "equalizer (of day and night)," agent noun from Latin aequare "make equal" (see equate).

meanings.definitionCn: 委任状；委员会；佣金，回扣；授权，委托

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 发送 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: also stair-case, 1620s, originally the enclosure of the stairs, from stair + case (n.2) in its sense "frame;" compare former window-case, door-case..

meanings.definitionCn: 〈口〉无理的， 轻率的， 轻浮的

etymology.story: "talkative and disrespectfully smart," see flippant..

meanings.definitionCn: 理性的， 理智的； 合理的

etymology.story: late 14c., "pertaining to reason;" mid-15c., "endowed with reason," from Old French racionel and directly from Latin rationalis "of or belonging to reason, reasonable," from ratio (genitive rationis) "reckoning, calculation, reason" (see ratio)..

meanings.definitionCn: 挑衅的， 煽动的， 刺激的； 挑逗的

morphology.mnemonic: 测试/证明 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "eliciting," from Middle French provocatif (15c.) and directly from Late Latin provocativus "calling forth," from provocat-, past participle stem of Latin provocare (see provoke).

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 发送 + ee

etymology.story: 1620s, from commit + -ee, or else a revival of Anglo-French commite, past participle of commettre "to commit," from Latin committere "to unite, connect" (see commit).

meanings.definitionCn: 摆出姿势， 装模作样
盘旋上升； 不断急剧地上升

etymology.story: 1640s, "constitutional element" (earlier "one of a group of persons," 1560s), from Latin componentem (nominative componens), present participle of componere "to put together" (see composite).

meanings.definitionCn: 套间； 一套家具； 套， 组， 系列

etymology.story: 1670s, "train of followers or attendants," from French suite, from Old French suite, sieute "act of following, attendance" (see suit (n.), which is an earlier borrowing of the same French word).

meanings.definitionCn: 顾问； 会诊医师， 专科医生

etymology.story: 1690s, of persons going to oracles, from consult + -ant.

meanings.definitionCn: 假扮，化装，伪装；掩盖，掩饰
用来伪装的东西； 伪装， 掩饰

etymology.story: 1620s, "one side of a multi-sided body," from French facette (12c., Old French facete), diminutive of face "face, appearance" (see face (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 心胸宽大的， 慷慨的； 自由的， 自由主义的

morphology.mnemonic: 自由 + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 外科， 外科手术； 手术室
调和； 协调； 和谐

etymology.story: late 14c., "combination of tones pleasing to the ear," from Old French harmonie, armonie "harmony," also the name of a musical instrument (12c.), from Latin harmonia, from Greek harmonia "agreement, concord of sounds," also as a proper name, the personification of music, literally "means of joining," used of ship-planks, etc., also "settled government, order," related to harmos "fastenings of a door; shoulder," from PIE *ar-ti-, from *ar- "to fit together" (see arm (n.1)).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 太阳/独自

meanings.definitionCn: 迎面遇到， 遭遇； 勇敢地面对， 正视； 使对质， 使当面对证

etymology.story: 1560s, "to stand in front of," from Middle French confronter (15c.), from Medieval Latin confrontare "assign limits, adjoin," from Latin com- "together" (see com-) + frontem (nominative frons) "forehead" (see front (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 致死的，致命的；不共戴天的；极度的，十足的

etymology.story: Old English deadlic "mortal, subject to death," also "causing death;" see dead + -ly (1).
1630s, from French computer, from Latin computare "to count, sum up, reckon together," from com- "with" (see com-) + putare "to reckon," originally "to prune" (see pave).

meanings.definitionCn: 聚集在一起，挤作一团；把身子蜷成一团，蜷缩
挤在一起的人； 一堆杂乱的东西

etymology.story: 1570s, "to heap or crowd together," probably from Low German hudern "to cover, to shelter" (of hens on chicks or nurses with children), from Middle Low German huden "to cover up," which is probably a frequentative form from Proto-Germanic *hud-, from PIE *keudh-, extended form of the root *(s)keu- "to cover, conceal" (see hide (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 骨骼； 框架， 骨干； 梗概， 提要

etymology.story: 1570s, from Modern Latin sceleton "bones, bony framework of the body," from Greek skeleton soma "dried-up body, mummy, skeleton," from neuter of skeletos "dried-up" (also, as a noun, "dried body, mummy"), from skellein "dry up, make dry, parch," from PIE root *skele- "to parch, wither" (see sclero-).

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + cut切割 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1550s, "act or fact of crossing," from Middle French intersection (14c.) and directly from Latin intersectionem (nominative intersectio) "a cutting asunder, intersection," noun of action from past participle stem of intersecare "intersect, cut asunder," from inter- "between" (see inter-) + secare "to cut" (see section (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 使生效， 履行， 实施
工具， 器具， 用具
分散， 赶散； 消散， 驱散
使分散，散开；使消散，消失

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin dispersus, past participle of dispergere "to scatter," from dis- "apart, in every direction" (see dis-) + spargere "to scatter" (see sparse).

meanings.definitionCn: 防止， 避免； 转移
想象， 幻想； 想象的产物

etymology.story: early 14c., "illusory appearance," from Old French fantaisie, phantasie "vision, imagination" (14c.), from Latin phantasia, from Greek phantasia "power of imagination; appearance, image, perception," from phantazesthai "picture to oneself," from phantos "visible," from phainesthai "appear," in late Greek "to imagine, have visions," related to phaos, phos "light," phainein "to show, to bring to light" (see phantasm).

meanings.definitionCn: 装运， 装船； 装载的货物

etymology.story: 1802, "act of shipping;" 1861, "that which is shipped;" see ship (v.) + -ment..

meanings.definitionCn: 恶毒的， 凶残的； 剧烈的， 严重的

etymology.story: late 14c., "unwholesome, impure, of the nature of vice, wicked, corrupting, pernicious, harmful;" of a text, "erroneous, corrupt," from Anglo-French vicious, Old French vicios "wicked, cunning, underhand; defective, illegal" (Modern French vicieux), from Latin vitiosus (Medieval Latin vicious) "faulty, full of faults, defective, corrupt; wicked, depraved," from vitium "fault" (see vice (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 网眼花边，透孔织品；鞋带，系带

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + tern + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French externe or directly from Latin externus "outside, outward" (from exterus; see exterior) + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 华丽的， 灿烂的， 绚丽的； 令人十分愉快的， 极好的
腐蚀， 侵蚀， 磨损； 削弱， 减少

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 咬，啮 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1540s, from Middle French erosion (16c.), from Latin erosionem (nominative erosio) "a gnawing away," noun of action from past participle stem of erodere "gnaw away" (see erosion).
"with condition that," early 15c., conjunction use of past participle of provide.

meanings.definitionCn: 仪器， 工具； 乐器

etymology.story: late 13c., "musical instrument, mechanical apparatus for producing musical sounds," from Old French instrument, enstrument "means, device; musical instrument" (14c., earlier estrument, 13c.) and directly from Latin instrumentum "a tool, an implement; means, furtherance; apparatus, furniture; ornament, dress, embellishment; a commission, authorization; a document," from instruere "arrange, furnish" (see instruct (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 透镜， 镜片； 镜头

etymology.story: 1690s, "glass to regulate light rays," from Latin lens (genitive lentis) "a lentil," on analogy of the double-convex shape.
late 14c., from Old French motif "moving" or directly from Medieval Latin motivus "moving, impelling," from past participle stem of movere "to move" (see move (v.))..
1778, "look in the face of," from French envisager "look in the face of," from en- "in" (see en- (1)) + visage "face" (see visage).

meanings.definitionCn: 摔跤； 斗争， 搏斗

etymology.story: Old English *wræstlian, frequentative of wræstan "to wrest" (see wrest) with -el (3).

morphology.mnemonic: 移动 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1873, from motivate + -ion.

meanings.definitionCn: 微小的， 少量的； 不重要的； 仅以微弱多数票获胜的； 记在页边的， 有旁注的

morphology.mnemonic: 狂热 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1570s, "written on the margin," from Medieval Latin marginalis, from Latin margo "edge, brink, border, margin" (see margin (n.)).

mid-12c., multeplier, "to cause to become many," from Old French multiplier, mouteplier (12c.) "increase, get bigger; flourish; breed; extend, enrich," from Latin multiplicare "to increase," from multiplex (genitive multiplicis) "having many folds, many times as great in number," from comb.

meanings.definitionCn: 赞成， 同意； 认可， 批准

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1680s, from approve + -al (2).
1640s, "involving many parts," from French multiple (14c.), from Late Latin multiplus "manifold," from Latin multi- "many, much" (see multi-) + -plus "-fold" (see -plus).

meanings.definitionCn: 达到高峰； 达到最大值
磁铁， 磁体； 有吸引力的人和事物
全神贯注，全力以赴；集中，聚集；浓缩

root.meaning: to rest as at a center

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + to rest as at a center + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: also exposé, "display of discreditable information," 1803, initially as a French word; noun use of past participle of French exposer "lay open" (see expose (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 多才多艺的， 有多种技能的； 有多种用途的， 多功能的， 万用的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French permissible (15c.) and directly from Medieval Latin permissibilis, from permiss-, past participle stem of Latin permittere (see permit (v.))..

Old English cancer "spreading sore, cancer" (also canceradl), from Latin cancer "a crab," later, "malignant tumor," from Greek karkinos, which, like the Modern English word, has three meanings: crab, tumor, and the zodiac constellation (late Old English), from PIE root *qarq- "to be hard" (like the shell of a crab); source also of Sanskrit karkatah "crab," karkarah "hard;" and perhaps cognate with PIE root *qar-tu- "hard, strong," source of English hard.

meanings.definitionCn: 溅泼声； 溅出的水； 斑点
注射， 给…注射； 注入， 引入， 投入
恋爱， 恋爱关系； 浪漫气氛， 传奇色彩； 爱情小说， 传奇故事

etymology.story: late 14c., "recite a narrative," from Old French romancier "narrate in French; translate into French," from romanz (see romance (n.)).

"collection of maps in a volume," 1636, first in reference to the English translation of "Atlas, sive cosmographicae meditationes de fabrica mundi" (1585) by Flemish geographer Gerhardus Mercator (1512-1594), who might have been the first to use this word in this way.
mid-14c., "not perceptible to sight, incapable of being seen," from Old French invisible (13c.), from Latin invisibilis "unseen, not visible," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + visibilis (see visible).

morphology.mnemonic: to heap, pile, load, fill, + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1560s, from Latin exaggerationem (nominative exaggeratio) "elevation, exaltation" (figurative), noun of action from past participle stem of exaggerare "amplify, magnify," literally "heap up" (see exaggerate)..

meanings.definitionCn: 用钉子钉， 用钉固定； 限定； 将…看成， 将…归入
杜撰出来的人； 神话

etymology.story: 1830, from French Mythe (1818) and directly from Modern Latin mythus, from Greek mythos "speech, thought, story, myth, anything delivered by word of mouth," of unknown origin.General sense of "untrue story, rumor" is from 1840..
1580s, from Latin concisus "cut off, brief," past participle of concidere "to cut off, cut up, cut through, cut to pieces," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + caedere "to cut" (see -cide).

meanings.definitionCn: 含意， 暗示， 暗指； 卷入， 牵连

morphology.mnemonic: in + 折叠/交织 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "action of entangling," from Latin implicationem (nominative implicatio) "an interweaving, an entanglement," noun of state from past participle stem of implicare "involve, entangle; embrace; connect closely, associate," from assimilated form of in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + plicare "to fold" (see ply (v.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 论文， 毕业论文； 论题， 论点

etymology.story: late 14c., "unaccented syllable or note," from Latin thesis "unaccented syllable in poetry," later (and more correctly) "stressed part of a metrical foot," from Greek thesis "a proposition," also "downbeat" (in music), originally "a setting down, a placing, an arranging; position, situation," from root of tithenai "to place, put, set," from PIE root *dhe- "to set, to put" (see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 热情友好的， 热诚的

morphology.mnemonic: 心 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., "of the heart," from Middle French cordial, from Medieval Latin cordialis "of or for the heart," from Latin cor (genitive cordis) "heart," from PIE root *kerd- (1) "heart" (see heart (n.)).
1540s, from Middle French timide "easily frightened, shy" (16c.) and directly from Latin timidus "fearful, afraid, cowardly," from timere "to fear," of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 改革， 改良， 改造； 改正， 改过自新

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 形状/形态

meanings.definitionCn: 安装， 设置； 使就职， 任命

etymology.story: also instal, formerly also enstall, early 15c., "place in (ecclesiastical) office by seating in an official stall," from Old French installer (14c.) or directly from Medieval Latin installare, from Latin in- "in" (see in- (2)) + Medieval Latin stallum "stall," from a Germanic source (compare Old High German stal "standing place;" see stall (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 接着发生， 接踵而来， 因而产生
指出， 指示； 迹象， 暗示

etymology.story: early 15c., "a sign, that which indicates," from Latin indicationem (nominative indicatio) "an indicating; valuation," noun of action from past participle stem of indicare "point out, show," from in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + dicare "proclaim" (see diction)..

meanings.definitionCn: 变得越来越大； 使更大
公牛； 雄兽； 买进证券投机图利者， 看涨的人

etymology.story: 1850, keramic, from Greek keramikos, from keramos "potter's clay, pottery, tiles," perhaps from a pre-Hellenic word.

meanings.definitionCn: 奇怪的， 异常的； 不舒服的， 眩晕的
接替的人或事物， 继任者

etymology.story: "one who comes after," late 13c., from Anglo-French successor and Old French successour "successor, heir" (12c., Modern French successeur), from Latin successor "follower, successor," agent noun from past participle stem of succedere "to come after" (see succeed)..

meanings.definitionCn: 启发， 开导； 开明， 文明

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + something used for igniting + 使变成/变得 + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: 1660s, "action of enlightening," from enlighten + -ment.

root.meaning: to enter into a book, record

morphology.mnemonic: to enter into a book, record + let

etymology.story: 1859, from book (n.) + diminutive ending -let..

meanings.definitionCn: 壮丽的， 华丽的； 极好的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Old French magnificent, a back-formation from Latin magnificentior, comparative of magnificus "great, elevated, noble, distinguished," literally "doing great deeds" (see magnificence)..

mid-15c., "walk unsteadily, reel" (intransitive), altered from stakeren (early 14c.), from a Scandinavian source (compare Old Danish stagra, Old Norse stakra "to push, shove, cause to reel," also "to stumble, stagger," perhaps literally "hit with a stick," from Proto-Germanic *stakon- "a stake," from PIE *steg- (1) "pole, stick." Cognate with Dutch staggelen "to stagger," German staggeln "to stammer." Transitive sense of "bewilder, amaze" first recorded 1550s; that of "arrange in a zig-zag pattern" is from 1856.

root.meaning: to affix an ID band to (a wild animal, etc.)

morphology.mnemonic: to affix an ID band to (a wild animal, etc.) + 行为/结果/集合

meanings.definitionCn: 碎片，碎屑；废金属；残羹剩饭；少量，点滴
综合病症， 综合症状； 一系列表现

etymology.story: "a number of symptoms occurring together," 1540s, from medical Latin, from Greek syndrome "concurrence of symptoms, concourse of people," from syndromos "place where several roads meet," literally "a running together," from syn- "with" (see syn-) + dromos "a running, course" (see dromedary).

"pull with a rope," Old English togian "to drag, pull," from Proto-Germanic *tugojanan (source also of Old English teon "to draw," Old Frisian togia "to pull about," Old Norse toga, Old High German zogon, German ziehen "to draw, pull, drag"), from PIE root *deuk- "to pull, draw" (source also of Latin ducere "to lead;" see duke (n.)).
Old English mædwe "meadow, pasture," originally "land covered in grass which is mown for hay;" oblique case of mæd (see mead (n.2))..

meanings.definitionCn: 精力充沛的， 充满活力的

etymology.story: 1650s, "powerful in operation," from Greek energetikos "active," from energein "to work, be in action, act upon" (see energy).

meanings.definitionCn: 拖， 拉， 托运； 一次获得的数量
有帮助； 有益； 有用
结果， 后果； 重要， 重大

etymology.story: late 14c., "inference, conclusion," from Old French consequence "result" (13c., Modern French conséquence), from Latin consequentia, from consequentem (nominative consequens), present participle of consequi "to follow after," from com- "with" (see com-) + sequi "to follow" (see sequel).

meanings.definitionCn: 线的， 直线的， 线状的； 长度的； 线性的

morphology.mnemonic: one's occupation, branch of business + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1640s, from French linéaire, from Latin linearis "belonging to a line," from linea "string, line" (see line (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 占优势的， 支配的， 统治的； 居高临下的， 高耸的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French dominant (13c.), from Latin dominantem (nominative dominans), present participle of dominari (see domination).

meanings.definitionCn: 首要的， 首位的， 首次的

etymology.story: 1711 in the political sense, a shortening of premier minister (1680s); see premier (adj.).

"to strip off," developed from Old English pilian "to peel, skin, decorticate, strip the skin or ring," and Old French pillier, both from Latin pilare "to strip of hair," from pilus "hair" (see pile (n.3)).

meanings.definitionCn: 要回； 开垦； 回收

etymology.story: early 14c., "call back a hawk to the glove," from Old French reclamer "to call upon, invoke; claim; seduce; to call back a hawk" (12c.) and directly from Latin reclamare "cry out against, contradict, protest, appeal," from re- "opposite, against" (see re-) + clamare "cry out" (see claim (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 谴责， 指责； 告发

etymology.story: early 14c., "announce," from Old French denoncier (12c., Modern French dénoncer), from Latin denuntiare "to announce, proclaim; denounce, menace; command, order," from de- "down" + nuntiare "proclaim, announce," from nuntius "messenger" (see nuncio).

"edge, rim," mid-15c., from Old French verge "twig, branch; measuring rod; penis; rod or wand of office" (12c.), hence, from the last sense, "scope, territory dominated" (as in estre suz la verge de "be under the authority of"), from Latin virga "shoot, rod, stick, slender green branch," of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 油酥面团， 酥皮糕点

etymology.story: mid-15c., "food made with paste," not originally limited to sweets, from Middle English paste (see paste (n.)) + -ry.

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + 有能力的，能干的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from dis- "do the opposite of" + ablen (v.) "to make fit" (see able).

meanings.definitionCn: 不考虑的， 不顾及的

root.meaning: treat with deferential regard or esteem

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + treat with deferential regard or esteem + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1620s (implied in irrespectively), "disrespectful," from assimilated form of in- (1) "not, opposite of" + respective in its sense of "regardful." Meaning "without taking account of particular circumstances or conditions" had developed by 1690s, from the notion of "not observing or noting with attention." In modern use it tends to be adverbial, in irrespective of, a use attested by c.

meanings.definitionCn: 实体， 独立存在体， 实际存在物

etymology.story: 1590s, from Late Latin entitatem (nominative entitas), from ens (genitive entis) "a thing," proposed by Caesar as present participle of esse "be" (see is), to render Greek philosophical term to on "that which is" (from neuter of present participle of einai "to be;" see essence).

morphology.mnemonic: 向前/支持 + 拥有/保持

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin prohibitus, past participle of prohibere "to hold back, restrain" (see prohibition).

meanings.definitionCn: 当政， 统治； 占主导地位
君主的统治，君主的统治时期

etymology.story: "to hold or exercise sovereign power," late 13c., from Old French regner "rule, reign" (12c.), from Latin regnare "have royal power, be king, rule, reign," from regnum "kingship, dominion, rule, realm," related to regere "to rule, to direct, keep straight, guide" (see regal).

meanings.definitionCn: 速度； 节奏， 行进速度

etymology.story: "relative speed of a piece of music," 1724, from Italian tempo, literally "time" (plural tempi), from Latin tempus "time, season, portion of time" (see temporal).
Old English foreseon "have a premonition," from fore- "before" + seon "to see, see ahead" (see see (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 把…联系在一起；使联合，结合；交往	[ə'səuʃiət]

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + socio + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: early 15c., "allied, connected, paired," from Latin associatus, past participle of associare (see associate (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 爬行， 蠕动； 缓慢行进
恳求，请求；申诉，答辩，辩护
为…辩护， 提出…为理由

etymology.story: mid-13c., "make a plea in court," from Anglo-French pleder, Old French plaidier, "plead at court" (11c.), from Medieval Latin placitare, from Late Latin placitum (see plea).
early 14c., kidenere, a word of unknown origin, perhaps a compound of Old English cwið "womb" (see bowel) + ey "egg" (see egg (n.)) in reference to the shape of the organ.

meanings.definitionCn: 闪亮， 闪烁； 闪现， 流露
有抱负的， 雄心勃勃的； 有野心的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin ambitiosus "going around to canvass for office," from ambitio (see ambition).

meanings.definitionCn: 学院的；学术的；纯理论的，不切实际的

etymology.story: 1580s, "relating to an academy," also "collegiate, scholarly," from Latin academicus "of the Academy," from academia (see academy).

meanings.definitionCn: 工作， 运行； 起作用
功能，职务；【数】函数；重大聚会
原料，库存品；股本，公债；世系，血统；汤汁；家畜，牲畜

etymology.story: in reference to conversation, literature, "recurring, commonplace" (as in stock phrase), 1738, figurative use from sense "kept in store for constant use" (1620s), from stock (n.2)..

meanings.definitionCn: 空虚感，寂寞感；真空，空白

etymology.story: "to clear" (some place, of something), c.

meanings.definitionCn: 比较， 对照； 比拟， 比喻

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French comparaison (12c.), from Latin comparationem (nominative comparatio), noun of action from past participle stem of comparare "make equal with, liken, bring together for a contest," literally "to couple together, to form in pairs," from com- "with" (see com-) + parare "prepare" (see pare)..

meanings.definitionCn: 蜂拥而出；洋溢，奔放；汹涌，奔腾
洋溢， 奔放； 急剧上升， 猛增； 浪涛般汹涌奔腾
爱国者， 爱国主义者

etymology.story: 1590s, "compatriot," from Middle French patriote (15c.) and directly from Late Latin patriota "fellow-countryman" (6c.), from Greek patriotes "fellow countryman," from patrios "of one's fathers," patris "fatherland," from pater (genitive patros) "father" (see father (n.)); with -otes, suffix expressing state or condition.
early 13c., "to touch or pat," perhaps from an Old English verb corresponding to tick (n.2), and perhaps ultimately echoic.

meanings.definitionCn: 基础， 根据； 原则

etymology.story: 1570s, "bottom or foundation (of something material)," from Latin basis "foundation," from Greek basis "a step, stand, base, that whereon one stands," from bainein "go, step" (see come).
early 15c., from Medieval Latin successivus "successive," from success-, stem of Latin succedere "to come after" (see succeed).
1840, from Medieval Latin quantificare, from Latin quantus "as much," correlative pronominal adjective (see quantity) + facere "to make" (see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 吸引， 引起…的兴趣； 引诱， 诱惑

morphology.mnemonic: 约束/义务 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1899, from paper (n.) + back (n.).
Old English yfel (see evil (adj.))..
1550s, from rare (adj.1) + -ly (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 爱国的， 有爱国心的， 显示爱国精神的

etymology.story: 1650s, "of one's own country," from French patriotique or directly from Late Latin patrioticus, from Greek patriotikos, from patriotes (see patriot).

meanings.definitionCn: 困难， 难题； 并发症

morphology.mnemonic: to make more complex + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French complication, from Latin complicationem (nominative complicatio), noun of action from past participle stem of complicare "to fold together, fold up, roll up," from com- "together" (see com-) + plicare "to fold, weave" (see ply (v.1)).
1560s, from Middle French combat (16c.), from combattre (see combat (v.))..
"an act of levying," early 15c., from Anglo-French leve, Old French levée "a raising, lifting; levying," noun use of fem.
1640s, from Medieval Latin intimidatus, past participle of intimidare "to frighten, make afraid," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + Latin timidus "fearful" (see timid).

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 做/制造 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., "serving to effect the intended purpose," from Old French effectif, from Latin effectivus "productive, effective," from effect-, stem of efficere (see effect (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 单一， 统一， 团结； 和睦， 协调
粗野的， 下流的； 庸俗的， 粗俗的

etymology.story: late 14c., "common, ordinary," from Latin vulgaris, volgaris "of or pertaining to the common people, common, vulgar, low, mean," from vulgus "the common people, multitude, crowd, throng," perhaps from a PIE root *wel- "to crowd, throng" (source also of Sanskrit vargah "division, group," Greek eilein "to press, throng," Middle Breton gwal'ch "abundance," Welsh gwala "sufficiency, enough") [not in Watkins].

1530s, "reversal of what is expected" (especially a fatal turning point in a drama), from Latin catastropha, from Greek katastrophe "an overturning; a sudden end," from katastrephein "to overturn, turn down, trample on; to come to an end," from kata "down" (see cata-) + strephein "turn" (see strophe).

meanings.definitionCn: 表达； 传递； 运送， 输送
设计， 构想； 图样； 企图

etymology.story: 1580s, from Middle French desseign "purpose, project, design," from Italian disegno, from disegnare "to mark out," from Latin designare "to mark out" (see design (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 巧合， 巧事； 一致， 符合

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 落入/巧合 + ance

meanings.definitionCn: 胸像， 半身像； 胸部， 胸围

etymology.story: "to burst," 1806, variant of burst (v.); for loss of -r-, compare ass (n.2).
"bony framework of the head," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 情绪异常激动的， 歇斯底里般的

morphology.mnemonic: hyster + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1610s, "characteristic of hysteria," from Latin hystericus "of the womb," from Greek hysterikos "of the womb, suffering in the womb," from hystera "womb" (see hysteria).

meanings.definitionCn: 小路， 跑道； 航道， 航线

etymology.story: Old English lane, lanu "narrow hedged-in road," common Germanic (cognates: Old Frisian lana, Middle Dutch lane, Dutch laan "lane, alley, avenue," Old Norse lön "small, oblong hayrick," in modern use "row of houses"), but of unknown origin.

1620s, from French soldiers' faulty separation of Middle French la munition into l'ammunition; from Latin munitionem (nominative munitio) "a fortifying" (see munition), and at first meaning all military supplies in general.

meanings.definitionCn: 首领， 长官； 酋长， 族长
相符合， 相一致， 相和谐； 授予， 赠与， 给予

etymology.story: late 13c., accourd, from Old French acord "agreement," a back-formation from acorder (see accord (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 最好的， 最著名的， 最重要的

morphology.mnemonic: for the (reason) that. + the best, extremely good

etymology.story: Middle English formest, from Old English fyrmest, formest "earliest, first, most prominent," from Proto-Germanic *furmista-/*frumista- (related to Old English fruma "beginning"), from PIE *pre-mo-, suffixed (superlative) form of root *per- (1) "forward, through; before; first" (see per) + additional superlative suffix -est.
1610s, from French consécutif (16c.), from Medieval Latin consecutivus, from Latin consecutus "following closely," past participle of consequi (see consequence).

meanings.definitionCn: 探索，查究，调查；用探针探查，探测
探针， 探测器； 探索， 调查
同化， 同化作用； 消化

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 相似/相同 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "act of assimilating," from Old French assimilacion, from Latin assimilationem (nominative assimilatio) "likeness, similarity," noun of action from past participle stem of assimilare (see assimilate).

meanings.definitionCn: 呻吟，抱怨；发出呻吟般的声音
呻吟， 抱怨； 呻吟般的声音

morphology.mnemonic: habitual diligence, effort + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1774, "resulting from labor," from French industriel, from Medieval Latin industrialis, from Latin industria "diligence, activity" (see industry).

early 14c., probably from Old Provençal veluet, from Vulgar Latin *villutittus, diminutive of Vulgar Latin *villutus "velvet," literally "shaggy cloth," from Latin villus "shaggy hair, nap of cloth, tuft of hair," probably a dialectal variant of vellus "fleece," from PIE *wel-no-, suffixed form of root *wel- (4) "to tear, pull" (see svelte)..
1580s, "having quantity," from Medieval Latin quantitativus, from stem of Latin quantitas (see quantity).

meanings.definitionCn: 稳定的，不变的；沉稳的，持重的
极好的， 极出色的； 了不起的； 极大的； 难以相信的； 异想天开的， 不实际的； 奇异的， 古怪的

etymology.story: late 14c., "existing only in imagination," from Middle French fantastique (14c.), from Medieval Latin fantasticus, from Late Latin phantasticus "imaginary," from Greek phantastikos "able to imagine," from phantazein "make visible" (middle voice phantazesthai "picture to oneself"); see phantasm.

meanings.definitionCn: 排水系统， 下水道； 排水， 放水

etymology.story: 1650s, from drain + -age..

morphology.mnemonic: 落于/发生 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story:
late 15c., "likely to happen," from Latin incidentem (nominative incidens), present participle of 
incidere "to happen, befall" (see incident (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 跛的， 瘸的； 站不住脚的， 差劲的； 蹩脚的
桶， 塑料杯， 纸杯； 盆， 洗澡盆， 浴缸

etymology.story: "open wooden vessel made of staves," late 14c., from Middle Low German, Middle Dutch, or Middle Flemish tubbe, of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 捕食； 折磨， 使烦恼
被捕食的动物，捕获物；受害者
器械， 器具， 仪器； 机构， 组织

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin apparatus "tools, implements, equipment; preparation, a preparing," noun of state from past participle stem of apparare "prepare," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + parare "make ready" (see pare)..

meanings.definitionCn: 陆军中尉； 海军上尉

etymology.story: late 14c., "one who takes the place of another," from Old French lieu tenant "substitute, deputy," literally "placeholder," from lieu "place" (see lieu) + tenant, present participle of tenir "to hold" (see tenant).

meanings.definitionCn: 拖船； 猛拉， 牵引
吸收； 吸引…的注意， 使全神贯注； 把…并入， 同化

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French absorber (Old French assorbir, 13c.), from Latin absorbere "to swallow up," from ab- "from" (see ab-) + sorbere "suck in," from PIE root *srebh- "to suck, absorb" (source also of Armenian arbi "I drank," Greek rhopheo "to sup greedily up, gulp down," Lithuanian srebiu "to drink greedily").

meanings.definitionCn: 关切的事，有关的事；关心，挂念；关系，关联；公司，企业
涉及， 有关； 使关心， 使担心
裂缝， 裂痕； 折断

morphology.mnemonic: 打破 + 行为/过程/结果

meanings.definitionCn: 契约， 协定， 条约

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French pacte "agreement, treaty, compact" (14c.), from Latin pactum "agreement, contract, covenant," noun use of neuter past participle of pacisci "to covenant, to agree, make a treaty," from PIE root *pag- "fix, join together, unite, make firm" (source also of Sanskrit pasa- "cord, rope," Avestan pas- "to fetter," Greek pegnynai "to fix, make firm, fast or solid," Latin pangere "to fix, to fasten," Slavonic paž "wooden partition," Old English fegan "to join," fon "to catch seize")..

morphology.mnemonic: notice, attention, reputation + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1560s, "explanation of a term," from Middle French notation and directly from Latin notationem (nominative notatio) "a marking, notation, designation; etymology; shorthand; explanation," noun of action from past participle stem of notare "to note" (see note (v.)).

mid-15c., "bound or obliged by law," probably from Anglo-French *liable, from Old French lier "to bind, tie up, fasten, tether; bind by obligation," from Latin ligare "to bind, to tie" (see ligament).
1922, originally of industrial processes; see re- + cycle (v.).

"cut an animal's tail," late 14c., from dok (n.) "fleshy part of an animal's tail" (mid-14c.), related to Old English -docca "muscle," from Proto-Germanic *dokko "something round, bundle" (source also of Old Norse dokka "bundle, girl," Danish dukke "doll," German Docke "small column, bundle, doll, smart girl").

Old English mersc, merisc "marsh, swamp," from Proto-Germanic *marisko (source also of Old Frisian and Old Saxon marsk "marsh," Middle Dutch mersch, Dutch mars, German Marsch, Danish marsk), probably from Proto-Germanic *mari- "sea" (see mere (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 成分， 要素， 元素； 基础， 纲要； 自然力
丰富的， 富裕的； 大量的， 充足的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French abundant and directly from Latin abundantem (nominative abundans) "overflowing," present participle of abundare "to overflow" (see abound).

1560s, back-formation from exclamation or else from Middle French exclamer (16c.), from Latin exclamare "cry out loud, call out," from ex- "out," or else here as an intensive prefix (see ex-) + clamare "cry, shout, call" (see claim (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 缄默的，无声的；哑的，不会说话的；不发音的
谦恭有礼； 有礼貌的举止

etymology.story: early 13c., curteisie, from Old French curteisie (Modern French courtoisie), from curteis "courteous" (see courteous).

late 14c., from Old French confesser (transitive and intransitive), from Vulgar Latin *confessare, from Latin confess-, past participle stem of confiteri "to acknowledge," from com- "together" (see com-) + fateri "to admit," akin to fari "speak," from PIE root *bha- (2) "to speak, tell, say" (see fame (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 抑制， 限制， 克制； 约束措施， 约束条件

etymology.story: "action of restraining; means of restraint," early 15c., from Old French restreinte, noun use of fem.

morphology.mnemonic: make physically foul + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1888, from pollute + -ant.
1590s, from Dutch bamboe, from Portuguese bambu, earlier mambu (16c.), probably from Malay samambu, though some suspect this is itself an imported word..

meanings.definitionCn: 输入；投入的资金，输入的数据

etymology.story: late 14c., "put on, impose," from in (adv.) + put (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 引导， 劝； 引起， 导致

etymology.story: formerly also enduce, late 14c., "to lead by persuasions or other influences," from Latin inducere "lead into, bring in, introduce, conduct; persuade; suppose, imagine," from in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + ducere "to lead" (see duke (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 远离/脱离 + 标准/规范 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1835, displaced older abnormous (1742) and rival anormal (1835) under influence of Latin abnormis "deviating from a rule," from ab- "off, away from" (see ab-) + norma "rule" (see norm).
1794, "extremely minute particle," from French molécule (1670s), from Modern Latin molecula, diminutive of Latin moles "mass, barrier" (see mole (3)).

meanings.definitionCn: 预防措施； 保证条款

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French diligent (14c.), from Latin diligentem "attentive, assiduous" (see diligence).

meanings.definitionCn: 娱乐， 文娱节目， 表演会； 招待， 款待， 请客

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + 持有/保持 + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: 1530s, "provision for support of a retainer; manner of social behavior," now obsolete, along with other 16c.

meanings.definitionCn: 居住， 定居； 存在， 在于

etymology.story: late 15c., "to settle," from Middle French resider (15c.) and directly from Latin residere "sit down, settle; remain behind, rest, linger; be left," from re- "back, again" (see re-) + sedere "to sit" (see sedentary).

meanings.definitionCn: 病房； 区； 受监护人

etymology.story: Old English weardian "to keep guard, watch, protect, preserve," from Proto-Germanic *wardon "to guard" (source also of Old Saxon wardon, Old Norse varða "to guard," Old Frisian wardia, Middle Dutch waerden "to take care of," Old High German warten "to guard, look out for, expect," German warten "to wait, wait on, nurse, tend"), from PIE *war-o- (see ward (n.)).
late 12c., a shortening of Old English adrædan, contraction of ondrædan "counsel or advise against," also "to dread, fear, be afraid," from on- "against" + rædan "to advise" (see read (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 详细检查， 仔细观察

etymology.story: early 15c., "a vote to choose someone to decide a question," from Late Latin scrutinium "a search, inquiry" (in Medieval Latin, "a mode of election by ballot"), from Latin scrutari "to examine, investigate, search," from PIE root *skreu- "to cut; cutting tool" (see shred (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 追逐， 追赶， 追求

etymology.story: late 14c., "an occurrence, incident, event," from Old French accident (12c.), from Latin accidentem (nominative accidens), present participle of accidere "happen, fall out, fall upon," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + cadere "fall" (see case (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 例行公事，惯例，惯常的程序

etymology.story: 1670s, from French routine "usual course of action, beaten path" (16c.), from route "way, path, course" (see route (n.)) + noun suffix -ine (see -ine (1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 攻击， 进攻； 突然发作
离婚； 分离， 脱离

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French divorcer, from divorce (see divorce (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 关税， 税率； 价目表， 收费表

etymology.story: 1590s, "arithmetical table," also "official list of customs duties on imports or exports; law regulating import duties," from Italian tariffa "tariff, price, assessment," Medieval Latin tarifa "list of prices, book of rates," from Arabic ta'rif "information, notification, a making known; inventory of fees to be paid," verbal noun from arafa "he made known, he taught." Sense of "classified list of charges made in a business" is recorded from 1757.

meanings.definitionCn: 生动的， 形象的； 绘画的， 文字的， 图表的

etymology.story: "vivid, describing accurately ," 1660s (graphically "vividly" is from 1570s), from Latin graphicus "picturesque," from Greek graphikos "of or for writing, belonging to drawing, picturesque," from graphe "writing, drawing," from graphein "to write" (see -graphy).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 伸展/趋向 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin retentionem (nominative retentio) "a retaining, a holding back," noun of action from past participle stem of retinere (see retain).

meanings.definitionCn: 创造， 创建； 创造的作品， 产物； 宇宙， 天地万物

etymology.story: late 14c., "action of creating, a created thing," from Old French creacion (14c., Modern French création) "creation, coming into being," from Latin creationem (nominative creatio) "a creating, a producing," in classical use "an electing, appointment, choice," noun of action from past participle stem of creare (see create).

meanings.definitionCn: 托辞， 伎俩； 躲闪， 躲避
组成部分， 原料； 要素

etymology.story: in early use also engredient, early 15c., "something forming part of a mixture," from Latin ingredientem (nominative ingrediens) "that which enters into" (a compound, recipe, etc.), present participle of ingredi "go in, enter," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + gradi "to step, go" (see grade (n.)).
late 14c., from Old French sorplus "remainder, extra" (12c., Modern French surplus), from Medieval Latin superplus "excess, surplus," from Latin super "over" (see super-) + plus "more" (see plus).

meanings.definitionCn: 巩固， 加强； 联为一体， 合并

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 太阳/独自 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1510s, "to compact into one body," from Latin consolidatus, past participle of consolidare "to make solid," from com- "together" (see com-) + solidare "to make solid" (see solid).

meanings.definitionCn: 信件， 函件； 通信， 通信联系； 符合， 一致， 相似

etymology.story: early 15c., "harmony, agreement," from Medieval Latin correspondentia, from correspondentem (nominative correspondens), present participle of correspondere (see correspond).

meanings.definitionCn: 振作精神， 恢复活力

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French refreschier "refresh, renew" (12c.; Modern French rafraîchir), from re- "again" (see re-) + fresche "fresh" (Modern French frais), from a Germanic source (such as Old High German frisc "fresh," see fresh (adj.)).

mid-13c., from Old English þeofð (West Saxon þiefð) "theft," from Proto-Germanic *theubitho (source also of Old Frisian thiufthe, Old Norse þyfð), from *theubaz "thief" (see thief) + abstract formative suffix *-itha (cognate with Latin -itatem; see -th (2))..

meanings.definitionCn: 揭露， 泄露， 透露

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French desclos "open, exposed, plain, explicit," past participle of desclore (Modern French déclore) "open, break open, unlock, reveal," from des- (see dis-) + clore "to close" (see close (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 使依附；贴上；系上；使依恋

etymology.story: 1550s, yeaghe "a light, fast-sailing ship," from Norwegian jaght or early Dutch jaght, both from Middle Low German jacht, shortened form of jachtschip "fast pirate ship," literally "ship for chasing," from jacht "chase," from jagen "to chase, hunt," from Old High German jagon, from Proto-Germanic *yago-, from PIE root *yek- (2) "to hunt" (source also of Hittite ekt- "hunting net").

meanings.definitionCn: 情妇；女主人；主妇；女教师；女能人

etymology.story: early 14c., "female teacher, governess," from Old French maistresse "mistress (lover); housekeeper; governess, female teacher" (Modern French maîtresse), fem.
early 15c., from Gaelic clann "family, stock, offspring," akin to Old Irish cland "offspring, tribe," both from Latin planta "offshoot" (see plant (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 经济/管理 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1570s, "pertaining to household management;" from economic + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 繁荣的,兴旺的,茂盛的,顺利的

morphology.mnemonic: agreeable to one's wishes, + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: early 15c., "tending to bring success," from prosper + -ous, or else from obsolete Middle French prospereus (15c.), from prosperer.

meanings.definitionCn: 磨光，擦亮；擦亮剂；优雅，精良
内阁；橱柜；展览艺术品的小陈列室

etymology.story: 1540s, "secret storehouse, treasure chamber," from Middle French cabinet "small room" (16c.), diminutive of Old French cabane "cabin" (see cabin); perhaps influenced by (or rather, from) Italian gabbinetto, diminutive of gabbia, from Latin cavea "stall, stoop, cage, den for animals" (see cave (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 眼/视 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1560s, from optic + -al (1).
1620s, from French évoquer or directly from Latin evocare "call out, rouse, summon" (see evocation).

meanings.definitionCn: 保险；保险费；保险契约；赔偿金

etymology.story: 1550s, "engagement to marry," a variant of ensurance "an assurance, pledge, guarantee," from Old French enseurance "assurance," from ensurer, from en- "make" (see en- (1)) + sur "safe, secure, undoubted" (see sure (adj.)).
late 15c., frequentative of Middle English dasen (see daze (v.)).

Old English þeoh, þeh, from Proto-Germanic *theuham (source also of Old Frisian thiach, Old Dutch thio, Dutch dij, Old Norse þjo, Old High German dioh), probably literally "the thick or fat part of the leg," from PIE *teuk- from root *teue- (2) "to swell" (source also of Lithuanian taukas, Old Church Slavonic tuku, Russian tuku "fat of animals;" Lithuanian tukti "to become fat;" Avestan tuma "fat;" Greek tylos "callus, lump," tymbos "burial mound, grave, tomb;" Old Irish ton "rump;" Latin tumere "to swell," tumulus "raised heap of earth," tumidus "swollen;"tumor "a swelling;" Middle Irish tomm "a small hill," Welsh tom "mound")..

meanings.definitionCn: 倾斜；翘起；以言词或文字抨击

etymology.story: "state of being inferior," 1590s, probably from Medieval Latin *inferioritas; see inferior + -ity.

1560s, from Middle French participant, from Latin participantem (nominative participans), present participle of participare "to share in, partake of" from particeps "sharing, partaking" (see participation)..

meanings.definitionCn: 刺眼；耀眼的光；受公众注目
编译；编制；编辑；[图情] 汇编

etymology.story: early 14c., from Old French compiler "compile, collect" (13c.), from Latin compilare "to plunder, rob," probably originally "bundle together, heap up;" hence "to pack up and carry off," from com- "together" (see com-) + pilare "to compress, ram down."
1590s, from Latin vitalitatem (nominative vitalitas) "vital force, life," from vitalis "pertaining to life" (see vital)..
1610s, "pertaining to movement," from French locomotif, from Latin loco "from a place" (ablative of locus "place;" see locus) + Late Latin motivus "moving" (see motive).

morphology.mnemonic: 给予 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French donacion (13c.), from Latin donationem (nominative donatio) "a presenting, giving," noun of action from past participle stem of donare "give as a gift," from donum "gift," from PIE *donum "gift" (source also of Sanskrit danam "offering, present," Old Church Slavonic dani "tribute," Lithuanian duonis "gift," Old Irish dan "gift, endowment, talent," Welsh dawn "gift"), from root *do- "to give" (see date (n.1))..

mid-14c., "firmness of resolve, mental equilibrium" (of persons), from Old French stablete, establete "firmness, solidity, stability; durability, constancy" (Modern French stabilité), from Latin stabilitatem (nominative stabilitas) "a standing fast, firmness," figuratively "security, steadfastness," from stabilis "steadfast, firm" (see stable (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 辐射；传播；流露；发射；广播

morphology.mnemonic: 射线/放射 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "having rays, furnished with rays, shining," 1660s, from Latin radiatus (see radiate (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 规定的， 特定的； 假设的

etymology.story: late 14c., "allotted, predestined," past participle adjective from give (v.).
late 15c., passe tyme "recreation, diversion, amusement, sport," from pass (v.) + time (n.).
early 15c., "follow up, pursue" (some course or action), from Latin prosecutus, past participle of prosequi "follow after, accompany; chase, pursue; attack, assail, abuse" (see pursue).
Old English *bæcce "something baked," from bacan "bake" (see bake (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 用言辞的,用文字的;口头的;动词的

morphology.mnemonic: 词 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., "dealing with words" (especially in contrast to things or realities), from Old French verbal (14c.) and directly from Late Latin verbalis "consisting of words, relating to verbs," from Latin verbum "word" (see verb).

meanings.definitionCn: 法务官； 初级律师， 事务律师

etymology.story: early 15c., "one who urges," from Middle French soliciteur, from soliciter (see solicit).

meanings.definitionCn: 接近， 邻近； 亲近

etymology.story: late 15c., from Middle French proximité "nearness" (14c.), from Latin proximitatem (nominative proximitas) "nearness, vicinity," from proximus "nearest, next; most direct; adjoining," figuratively "latest, most recent; next, following; most faithful," superlative of prope "near" (see propinquity)..
early 15c., "full of light," from Latin luminosus "shining, full of light," from lumen (genitive luminis) "light," related to lucere "to shine" (see light (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 鞭打；冲击；摆动；扎捆；煽动；讽刺
鞭打；睫毛；鞭子；责骂；讽刺

etymology.story: late 14c., "to foreordain," from Old French prédestiner (12c.) "predestine, ordain" (of God) and directly from Latin praedestinare "determine beforehand" (see predestination).
mid-14c., from Old French destinée (12c.) "purpose, intent, fate, destiny; that which is destined," noun use of fem.
1650s, from French capsule "a membranous sac" (16c.), from Latin capsula "small box or chest," diminutive of capsa "box, case, chest" (see case (n.2)).
1550s, from Medieval Latin conceptum "draft, abstract," in classical Latin "(a thing) conceived," from concep-, past participle stem of concipere "to take in" (see conceive).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 站立/设置 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1840, from German Ozon, coined in 1840 by German chemist Christian Friedrich Schönbein (1799-1868) from Greek ozon, neuter present participle of ozein "to smell" (see odor).

meanings.definitionCn: 模糊的，不明确的，无定限的

etymology.story: 1520s, "not precise, vague," from Latin indefinitus "indefinite," from in- "not, opposite of, without" (see in- (1)) + definitus, past participle of definire (see define).

1590s, from Middle French désastre (1560s), from Italian disastro "ill-starred," from dis-, here merely pejorative (see dis-) + astro "star, planet," from Latin astrum, from Greek astron (see star (n.)).

1873, oecology, "branch of science dealing with the relationship of living things to their environments," coined in German by German zoologist Ernst Haeckel (1834-1919) as Ökologie, from Greek oikos "house, dwelling place, habitation" (see villa) + -logia "study of" (see -logy).

meanings.definitionCn: 鸡尾酒； 餐前开胃小吃； 混合物

etymology.story: first attested 1806; H.L.

meanings.definitionCn: 大会,会议;惯例,常规,习俗;公约,协定

etymology.story: early 15c., "a formal agreement," also "a formal meeting or convention" (of rulers, etc.), also "a secret agreement," from Middle French convention and directly from Latin conventionem (nominative conventio) "meeting, assembly, covenant," noun of action from past participle stem of convenire (see convene)..

early 14c., "discomfort, inconvenience," from Old French desaise "lack, want; discomfort, distress; trouble, misfortune; disease, sickness," from des- "without, away" (see dis-) + aise "ease" (see ease).
late 14c., notwiþstondynge, from not + present participle of the verb withstand.

root.meaning: device that breaks an electrical circuit

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + device that breaks an electrical circuit + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 15c., from refuse + -al (2)..

meanings.definitionCn: 一次性的； 可动用的

etymology.story: 1640s, "that may be done without;" see dispose + -able.
nonmetallic element, 1817, coined by British chemist Thomas Thomson from silica (silicon dioxide), from which it was isolated.
1550s, from Middle French récipient (16c.) and directly from Latin recipientem (nominative recipiens), present participle of recipere (see receive).
late 15c., "public welfare, general good," from common (adj.) + wealth (n.); meaning "the state" is attested from 1510s; applied specifically to the government of England in the period 1649-1660..
unit of electrical power, 1882, in honor of James Watt (1736-1819), Scottish engineer and inventor.

meanings.definitionCn: 声名狼藉的，臭名昭著的

etymology.story: 1540s, "publicly known," from Medieval Latin notorius "well-known, commonly known," from Latin notus "known," past participle of noscere "come to know" (see know).

morphology.mnemonic: assassin + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 引出，预先提出；作为…的前提
前提；上述各项；房屋连地基
马戏团,杂技团;马戏场,杂技场

etymology.story: late 14c., in reference to ancient Rome, from Latin circus "ring, circular line," which was applied by Romans to circular arenas for performances and contests and oval courses for racing (especially the Circus Maximus), from or cognate with Greek kirkos "a circle, a ring," from PIE *kirk- from root *(s)ker- (3) "to turn, bend" (see ring (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 承租人；房客；佃户；居住者

morphology.mnemonic: 持有/保持 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: early 14c., "person who holds lands by title or by lease," from Anglo-French tenaunt (late 13c.), Old French tenant "possessor; feudal tenant" (12c.), noun use of present participle of tenir "to hold," from Latin tenere "hold, keep, grasp" (see tenet).

meanings.definitionCn: 作为结果而发生的;合成的
害虫；有害之物；讨厌的人

etymology.story: 1550s (in imprecations, "a pest upon ____," etc.), "plague, pestilence," from Middle French peste (1530s), from Latin pestis "deadly contagious disease; a curse, bane," of uncertain origin.

morphology.mnemonic: 脚/儿童 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "flour, eggs, and milk beaten together," late 14c., from Old French batteure "a beating," from Latin battuere "to beat, knock" (see batter (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 最高的；至高的；最重要的

etymology.story: 1520s, from Middle French suprême (15c.) and directly from Latin supremus "highest," superlative of superus "situated above," from super "above" (see super-).

late 13c., from Old French ostage, hostage "kindness, hospitality; residence, dwelling; rent, tribute; compensation; guarantee, pledge, bail; person given as security or hostage" (11c., Modern French ôtage), which is of uncertain origin.
late 14c., "state of being earlier," from Old French priorite (14c.), from Medieval Latin prioritatem (nominative prioritas) "fact or condition of being prior," from Latin prior (see prior (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 斜坡；倾斜；斜率；扛枪姿势
引进; 嵌入; 插入;

etymology.story: "narrow opening into a coast, arm of the sea," 1570s, said by old sources to be originally a Kentish term; a special use of Middle English inlate "passage or opening by which an enclosed place may be entered" (c.
1560s, from Middle French métallique or directly from Latin metallicus, from Greek metallikos, from metallon (see metal)..

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + gener + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1550s, back-formation from regeneration or else from Latin regeneratus, past participle of regenerare "bring forth again" (see regeneration).

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + 给予/分配 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French distribution (13c.) and directly from Latin distributionem (nominative distributio) "a division, distribution," noun of action from past participle stem of distribuere "deal out in portions," from dis- "individually" + tribuere "assign, allot" (see tribute)..

mid-15c., "concealed, secret," from Latin latentem (nominative latens) "lying hid, concealed, secret, unknown," present participle of latere "lie hidden, lurk, be concealed," from PIE *late-, suffixed form of root *lādh- "to be hidden" (source also of Greek lethe "forgetfulness, oblivion," Old Church Slavonic lajati "to lie in wait for").

meanings.definitionCn: 极好的；极其的，非常的；可怕的

etymology.story: 1660s, "frightening," from Latin terrificus "causing terror or fear, frightful," from terrere "fill with fear" (see terrible) + root of facere "to make" (see factitious).
1829, from stand (v.) + point (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 最初的,初级的;首要的,主要的,基本的

morphology.mnemonic: 第一/首要 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1861, American English, short for primary election (1792, with reference to France; in a U.S.

meanings.definitionCn: 海盗；盗版；侵犯专利权者
顽固的；顽强的；难处理的

etymology.story: late 14c., of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 消耗；吃完，喝光；(with)使着迷；烧毁

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French consumer "to consume" (12c.) and directly from Latin consumere "to use up, eat, waste," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + sumere "to take," from sub- "under" + emere "to buy, take" (see exempt (adj.))..
"round, vaulted roof," 1650s, from French dome (16c.), from Provençal doma, from Greek doma "house, housetop" (especially a style of roof from the east), related to domos "house" (see domestic).

meanings.definitionCn: 层，层次；膜；[植]压条；放置者，计划者
把…分层堆放；借助压条法；生根繁殖；将（头发）剪成不同层次

etymology.story: "combustible cord or tube for lighting an explosive device," also fuze, 1640s, from Italian fuso, literally "spindle" (the ignition device so called for its shape, because the originals were long, thin tubes filled with gunpowder), from Latin fusus "a spindle," which is of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 保证；担保；使确信；弄清楚

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French asseurer (12c., Modern French assurer) "to reassure, calm, protect, to render sure," from Vulgar Latin *assecurar, from Latin ad- "to" (see ad-) + securus "safe, secure" (see secure (adj.)).

1510s, from French prétexte, from Latin praetextum "a pretext, outward display," noun use of neuter past participle of praetexere "to disguise, cover," literally "weave in front" (for sense, compare pull the wool over (someone's) eyes); from prae- "in front" (see pre-) + texere "to weave," from PIE root *teks- "to weave, to make" (see texture (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 象牙；象牙色， 乳白色

etymology.story: early 14c., "subject or topic on which a person writes or speaks," from Old French tesme (13c., with silent -s- "indicating vowel length" [OED], Modern French thème) and directly from Latin thema "a subject, thesis," from Greek thema "a proposition, subject, deposit," literally "something set down," from root of tithenai "put down, place," from PIE *dhe-mn, from root *dhe- "to put, to do" (see factitious).

1829, from French solidarité "communion of interests and responsibilities, mutual responsibility," a coinage of the "Encyclopédie" (1765), from solidaire "interdependent, complete, entire," from solide (see solid (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 摔倒；倒塌；滚动；打滚；仓惶地行动
亲切的,客气的;宽厚的,仁慈的

morphology.mnemonic: to show favor + 具有…性质的

meanings.definitionCn: 了不起的；非常轰动的；出色的

morphology.mnemonic: gaudily alluring + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: mid-15c., "entirely, purely," from mere (adj.) + -ly (2).

late 14c., from Old French contradiction or directly from Latin contradictionem (nominative contradictio) "a reply, objection, counterargument," noun of action from past participle stem of contradicere, in classical Latin contra dicere "to speak against," from contra "against" (see contra) + dicere "to say, speak" (see diction)..

meanings.definitionCn: 财富， 财产； 丰富

etymology.story: mid-13c., "happiness," also "prosperity in abundance of possessions or riches," from Middle English wele "well-being" (see weal (n.1)) on analogy of health..
late 14c., earlier henne forth (late Old English); see hence + forth..

late 14c., "bag, knapsack," of uncertain origin, probably from an unrecorded Old North French *walet "roll, knapsack," or similar Germanic word in Anglo-French or Old French, from Proto-Germanic *wall- "roll," from PIE *wel- (3) (see volvox).

meanings.definitionCn: 有效的,效率高的;有能力的,能胜任的

etymology.story: late 14c., "making, producing immediate effect, active, effective," from Old French efficient and directly from Latin efficientem (nominative efficiens) "effective, efficient, producing, active," present participle of efficere "work out, accomplish" (see effect).

meanings.definitionCn: 低语；低语声；低声抱怨；潺潺声

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French murmurer "murmur, grouse, grumble" (12c.), from murmur "rumbling noise" (see murmur (n.)).

late 14c., "fierce passion," from Old French furie, fuire "rage, frenzy" (14c.), from Latin furia "violent passion, rage, madness," from or related to furere "to rage, be mad," which is of uncertain origin.

morphology.mnemonic: lowest or worst of anything + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French eternel "eternal," or directly from Late Latin aeternalis, from Latin aeternus "of an age, lasting, enduring, permanent, everlasting, endless," contraction of aeviternus "of great age," from aevum "age" (see eon).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + 强壮/价值 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: early 15c., "having great power or force," from Latin praevalentem (nominative praevalens) "of superior strength; mighty," present participle of praevalere "to be more able" (see prevail).

Old English clingan "hold fast, adhere closely; congeal, shrivel" (strong verb, past tense clang, past participle clungen), from Proto-Germanic *klingg- (source also of Danish klynge "to cluster;" Old High German klinga "narrow gorge;" Old Norse klengjask "press onward;" Danish klinke, Dutch klinken "to clench;" German Klinke "latch").

meanings.definitionCn: （塑料或金属的）夹子；回纹针；修剪；剪报

横梁；光线；电波；船宽；[计量] 秤杆
发送；以梁支撑；用…照射；流露

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 强壮/价值 + able

etymology.story: 1570s, "above value, too valuable for exact estimate," from in- (1) "not" + value (v.) "estimate the worth of" + -able.

morphology.mnemonic: 塑造，虚构 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., ficcioun, "that which is invented or imagined in the mind," from Old French ficcion "dissimulation, ruse; invention, fabrication" (13c.) and directly from Latin fictionem (nominative fictio) "a fashioning or feigning," noun of action from past participle stem of fingere "to shape, form, devise, feign," originally "to knead, form out of clay," from PIE *dheigh- "to build, form, knead" (source also of Old English dag "dough;" see dough).
late 14c., from Old French cocombre (13c., Modern French concombre), from Latin cucumerem (nominative cucumis), perhaps from a pre-Italic Mediterranean language.

meanings.definitionCn: 航空；飞行术；飞机制造业

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1866, from French aviation, noun of action from stem of Latin avis "bird" (see aviary).

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + notice, attention, reputation

etymology.story: 1590s, from Middle French dénoter (14c.), from Latin denotare "denote, mark out," from de- "completely" (see de-) + notare "to mark" (see note (v.)).

1570s, "intensity of expression," from Latin emphasis, from Greek emphasis "an appearing in, outward appearance;" in rhetoric, "significance, indirect meaning," from emphainein "to present, exhibit, display, let (a thing) be seen; be reflected (in a mirror), become visible," from assimilated form of en "in" (see en- (2)) + phainein "to show" (see phantasm).

meanings.definitionCn: 繁殖， 增殖； 传播， 宣传， 使普及

etymology.story: 1560s, "to cause to multiply," from Latin propagatus, past participle of propagare "to set forward, extend, procreate" (see propagation).

meanings.definitionCn: 威严；最高权威，王权；雄伟；权威

morphology.mnemonic: 狂热 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1620s, from Medieval Latin necessitatus, past participle of necessitare "to render necessary," from Latin necessitas (see necessity).

meanings.definitionCn: 正统的；传统的；惯常的；东正教的

etymology.story: mid-15c., of opinions, faith, from Late Latin orthodoxus, from Greek orthodoxos "having the right opinion," from orthos "right, true, straight" (see ortho-) + doxa "opinion, praise," from dokein "to seem," from PIE root *dek- "to take, accept" (see decent).

meanings.definitionCn: 惯常的；习惯的，适应的(一般作表语)

etymology.story: late 15c., "made customary, habitual," past participle adjective from accustom (v.)..

early 15c., from Middle French obtenir "acquire, obtain" (14c.), from Latin obtinere "hold, hold fast, take hold of, get possession of, acquire," from ob "to" (though perhaps intensive in this case; see ob-) + tenere "to hold" (see tenet).
1590s, after French homme d'état; see state (n.1) + man (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 大头钉, 粗缝, 行动方针, 食物

车间；研讨会；工场；讲习班

etymology.story: 1580s, from work (n.) + shop (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: [法] 非法的；违法的；违反规则的

etymology.story: 1630s, from French illégal (14c.) or directly from Medieval Latin illegalis, from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + Latin legalis (see legal).
late 13c., cors "body," from Old French cors "body, person, corpse, life" (9c.), from Latin corpus "body" (see corporeal).

late 14c., "pertaining to form or arrangement;" also, in philosophy and theology, "pertaining to the form or essence of a thing," from Old French formal, formel "formal, constituent" (13c.) and directly from Latin formalis, from forma "a form, figure, shape" (see form (n.)).

root.meaning: stand in some relation; have reference or respect

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + stand in some relation; have reference or respect

etymology.story: 1742, back-formation from correlation, or else a verbal use of the noun.
mid-15c., "to change, transform," from Latin commutare "to often change, to change altogether," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + mutare "to change" (see mutable).

meanings.definitionCn: 维持；支撑，承担；忍受；供养；证实

morphology.mnemonic: 射线/放射 + …的人/处于…状态

1540s, from Middle French revenge, back-formation from revengier (see revenge (v.))..

early 15c., "to sign at the bottom of a document," from Latin subscribere "write, write underneath, sign one's name; register," also figuratively "assent, agree to, approve," from sub "underneath" (see sub-) + scribere "write" (see script (n.)).
also distil, late 14c., from Old French distiller (14c.), from Latin distillare "trickle down in minute drops," from dis- "apart" (see dis-) + stillare "to drip, drop," from stilla "drop."

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + to rest as at a center + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1630s, "action of bringing to a center," noun of action from verb concentrate (v.).

morphology.mnemonic: 射线/放射 + 有…倾向的

"take (something) ill; be in some degree angry or provoked at," c.

etymology.story: "a step in walking," especially a long one, from Old English stride "a stride, a step," from the root of stride (v.).

etymology.story: 1520s, from Latin ambiguus "having double meaning, shifting, changeable, doubtful," adjective derived from ambigere "to dispute about," literally "to wander," from ambi- "about" (see ambi-) + agere "drive, lead, act" (see act (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: in + 推/驱动 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: early 15c., originally in reference to medicine that reduces swelling or humors, from Medieval Latin impulsivus, from Latin impuls-, past participle stem of impellere "strike against, push against" (see impel).
early 15c., "that produces a (physical) quality," from Medieval Latin qualitativus "relating to quality," from stem of Latin qualitas "a quality, property, nature" (see quality).

etymology.story: Old English milde "gentle, merciful," from Proto-Germanic *milthjaz- (source also of Old Norse mildr, Old Saxon mildi, Old Frisian milde, Middle Dutch milde, Dutch mild, Old High German milti, German milde "mild," Gothic mildiþa "kindness"), from PIE *meldh-, from root *mel- "soft," with derivatives referring to soft or softened materials (source also of Greek malthon "weakling," myle "mill;" Latin molere "to grind;" Old Irish meldach "tender;" Sanskrit mrdh "to neglect," also "to be moist").

1530s, in reference to subjects of sovereigns or governments, from Middle French loyal, from Old French loial, leal "of good quality; faithful; honorable; law-abiding; legitimate, born in wedlock," from Latin legalem, from lex "law." In most cases it has displaced Middle English leal, which is from the same French source.

meanings.definitionCn: 瘫痪， 麻痹， 中风

etymology.story: 1520s, from Latin paralysis, from Greek paralysis "paralysis, palsy," literally "loosening," from paralyein "disable, enfeeble," from para- "beside" (see para- (1)) + lyein "loosen, untie" (see lose).

etymology.story: "woo, offer homage," as one does at court, 1570s; see court (n.).

early 13c., from Old French chapele (12c., Modern French chapelle), from Medieval Latin cappella "chapel, sanctuary for relics," literally "little cape," diminutive of Late Latin cappa "cape" (see cap (n.)); by tradition, originally in reference to the sanctuary in France in which the miraculous cape of St.

etymology.story: 1540s, from Latin subsidiarius "belonging to a reserve, of a reserve, reserved; serving to assist or supplement," from subsidium "a help, aid, relief, troops in reserve" (see subsidy).
1610s, from Latin census "the enrollment of the names and property assessments of all Roman citizens," originally past participle of censere "to assess" (see censor (n.)).
1540s, variant spelling of corps (q.v.).
1660s, via French attitude (17c.), from Italian attitudine "disposition, posture," also "aptness, promptitude," from Late Latin aptitudinem (nominative aptitudo; see aptitude).

亲戚；相关物；[语] 关系词；亲缘植物

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin depictus, past participle of depingere "to portray, paint, sketch; describe, imagine," from de- "down" (see de-) + pingere "to paint" (see paint (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 蹦跳,跳跃；跳舞；一次飞行的距离

morphology.mnemonic: in + volve + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: 1706, from involve + -ment..

meanings.definitionCn: 润滑；涂油；起润滑剂作用

etymology.story: 1620s, "to make slippery or smooth" (especially by the application of an oil), from Latin lubricatus, past participle of lubricare "to make slippery or smooth," from lubricus "slippery" (see lubricant (adj.)).
"time limit," 1920, American English newspaper jargon, from dead (adj.) + line (n.).

1530s, originally "sweetheart," applied to either sex, from Dutch boel "lover; brother," probably a diminutive of Middle Dutch broeder "brother" (compare Middle High German buole "brother," source of German Buhle "lover;" see brother (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: [电子] 芯片；筹码；碎片；(食物的) 小片; 薄片

etymology.story: early 15c., "to chip" (intransitive, of stone); from Old English forcippian "to pare away by cutting, cut off," verbal form of cipp "small piece of wood" (see chip (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 中世纪的,中古(时代)的,老式的,原始的

etymology.story: 1827, "pertaining to or suggestive of the Middle Ages," coined in English from Latin medium "the middle" (see medium (n.)) + aevum "age" (see eon)..

meanings.definitionCn: 油轮；运油飞机；油槽车；坦克手

etymology.story: "ship for carrying oil or other liquid cargo," 1900, from tank (n.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 卷入,陷入,连累;包含,含有,涉及

etymology.story: late 14c., "envelop, surround; make cloudy or obscure," from Old French involver and directly from Latin involvere "envelop, surround, overwhelm," literally "roll into," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + volvere "to roll" (see volvox).

"small hand-held firearm," 1570s, from Middle French pistole "short firearm" (1566), of uncertain origin, sometimes said to be from German Pistole, from Czech pis'tala "firearm," literally "tube, pipe," from pisteti "to whistle," of imitative origin, related to Russian pischal "shepherd's pipe."

But earlier English form pistolet (1550) is said to be from Middle French pistolet "a small firearm," also "a small dagger," which may be the literal sense; though some connect this word with Italian pistolese, in reference to Pistoia, town in Tuscany noted for gunsmithing.

meanings.definitionCn: 不漏水的, 无懈可击的

etymology.story: also water-tight, late 14c., from water (n.1) + tight (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 击退；抵制；使厌恶；使不愉快

etymology.story: early 15c., "to drive away, remove," from Old French repeller or directly from Latin repellere "to drive back," from re- "back" (see re-) + pellere "to drive, strike" (see pulse (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 系列，连续；[电] 串联；级数；丛书

etymology.story: 1610s, "a number or set of things of one kind arranged in a line," from Latin series "row, chain, series, sequence, succession," from serere "to join, link, bind together, arrange, attach, put; join in speech, discuss," from PIE root *ser- (3) "to line up, join" (source also of Sanskrit sarat- "thread," Greek eirein "to fasten together in rows," Gothic sarwa (plural) "armor, arms," Old Norse sörve "necklace of stringed pearls," Old Irish sernaid "he joins together," Welsh ystret "row").

meanings.definitionCn: [数] 极大，最大限度；最大量
最高的；最多的；最大极限的

morphology.mnemonic: greatest premise, greatest among propositions + ium

meanings.definitionCn: 富于想象的；有创造力的

etymology.story: late 14c., ymaginatyf, "pertaining to imagination; forming images, given to imagining," from Old French imaginatif and directly from Medieval Latin imaginativus, from imaginat-, stem of Latin imaginari "picture to oneself" (see imagine).

1570s, originally used of the great auk of Newfoundland (now extinct), shift in meaning to the Antarctic bird (which looks something like it, found by Drake in Magellan's Straits in 1578) is from 1580s.

meanings.definitionCn: 描述,表示;代表,代理;阐明,说明

etymology.story: late 14c., "to bring to mind by description," also "to symbolize, serve as a sign or symbol of; serve as the type or embodiment of;" from Old French representer "present, show, portray" (12c.), from Latin repraesentare "make present, set in view, show, exhibit, display," from re-, intensive prefix (see re-), + praesentare "to present," literally "to place before" (see present (v.)).

1610s, " to enter or be put in rivalry with," from Middle French compéter "be in rivalry with" (14c.), or directly from Late Latin competere "strive in common," in classical Latin "to come together, agree, to be qualified," later, "strive together," from com- "together" (see com-) + petere "to strive, seek, fall upon, rush at, attack" (see petition (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 宽容的；容忍的；有耐药力的

morphology.mnemonic: 忍受/容忍 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1784, "free from bigotry or severity in judging others," from French tolérant (16c.), and directly from Latin tolerantem (nominative tolerans), present participle of tolerare "to bear, endure, tolerate" (see toleration).

meanings.definitionCn: 紧急情况,突然事件,非常时刻

etymology.story: "unforeseen occurrence requiring immediate attention," 1630s, from Latin emergens, present participle of emergere "to rise out or up" (see emerge).

meanings.definitionCn: 人与人之间的, 人际关系的

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + person + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1580s, "fit to be an example," from Middle French exemplaire, from Late Latin exemplaris "that serves as an example, pattern, or motto," from exemplum "example, pattern, model" (see example).

meanings.definitionCn: 活动停止期间；临时解雇；操作停止；失业期

etymology.story: also lay-off, lay off; 1889, "rest, relaxation, respite;" from the verbal phrase; see lay (v.) + off (adv.).

meanings.definitionCn: 可怜的；卑鄙的；令人苦恼或难受的

1530s, "freedom from exaggeration, self-control," from Middle French modestie or directly from Latin modestia "moderation, sense of honor, correctness of conduct," from modestus "moderate, keeping measure, sober, gentle, temperate," from modus "measure, manner" (see mode (n.1)).
1570s, "marked by deliberation," from Latin consideratus, past participle of considerare (see consider).

meanings.definitionCn: 魅力；魔法；迷人的美（等于glamour）

also sight-seeing, 1821, from sight (see sights) + present participle of see (v.).

late Old English planete, from Old French planete (Modern French planète), from Late Latin planeta, from Greek planetes, from (asteres) planetai "wandering (stars)," from planasthai "to wander," of unknown origin, possibly from PIE *pele- (2) "flat, to spread" on notion of "spread out." So called because they have apparent motion, unlike the "fixed" stars.
